demos.text: Of course, as you develop out your DAGs they are going to get increasingly complex, so we provide a few ways to modify these DAG views to make them easier to understand.
demos.reasoning: The passage has the concise, conversational style of human-written technical documentation, with a specific reference to DAG views and a natural explanatory transition. It does not show strong indicators of synthetic generation, such as excessive structure or generic elaboration.

demos.text: In general, try to keep the topology (that is, the layout) of your DAG tasks reasonably stable; dynamic DAGs tend to work better for loading configuration values dynamically or adjusting operator options as needed.
demos.reasoning: The passage has a polished, generalized instructional tone, with an explanatory parenthetical and some redundant phrasing (“dynamic DAGs” and “dynamically”) characteristic of AI-generated paraphrasing.

signature.instructions: Decide whether the supplied passage was generated by an AI.

fields.description: True for AI-generated text; otherwise False
